source: Bitcoin's price is influenced by supply and demand, investor and user sentiments, government regulations, and media hype [Source: https://www.investopedia.com/articles/investing/052014/why-bitcoins-value-so-volatile.asp]
Regulatory announcements can trigger unpredictable effects on Bitcoin's price [Source: https://www.vaneck.com/us/en/blogs/digital-assets/bitcoin-volatility/]
Bitcoin's price is influenced by market sentiment, news, social media, and public opinion [Source: https://support.bitcoin.com/en/articles/4363506-why-is-the-bitcoin-price-constantly-changing]
Bitcoin price fluctuations are affected by adoption by companies, investment firms, or governments [Source: https://www.fidelity.com/learning-center/trading-investing/bitcoin-price]
Bitcoin's price fluctuates due to its perceived store of value versus fiat currency [Source: https://appinventiv.com/blog/understanding-cryptocurrency-price-shift-in-market/]
Bitcoin suffers from liquidity problems, contributing to its sharp volatility [Source: https://www.fxcm.com/markets/insights/what-causes-volatility-in-bitcoin/]
